summary: Kenneth Suna discusses Roth IRA allocation strategy and whether holding multiple ETFs is advisable. The video focuses on tax-optimization principles for long-term retirement accounts and portfolio diversification, with no discussion of options selling, premium collection, or income strategies relevant to Brian Norton's Premium Desk covered call/cash-secured put wheel strategy.
takeaways: Prioritize tax-advantaged accounts for highest-growth assets like tech funds (FTECH up 658% vs S&P 500 up 275% over 10 years)
Avoid overlap between ETFs in Roth IRA—use overlap analysis tools to identify redundancy (example: QQQ and SCHG both have 43-51% technology exposure)
Multiple ETF positions create smaller slices of each holding; all-in single ETF approach acceptable if intentional and paired with additional investments in taxable accounts
Tax-free growth on high-volatility assets should drive Roth IRA allocation decisions over dividend-focused holdings
title: Do you NEED more than one etf in your Roth IRA??
